title: Recognizing the significance of "Community College Month" in April 2025 as a celebration of the more than 1,000 community colleges throughout the United States that support access to higher education, workforce training, and more, and broadly sustain and advance the economic prosperity of the United States.
plain_summary: This bill officially recognizes April 2025 as Community College Month to celebrate America's community colleges. The bill highlights that there are more than 1,000 community colleges across the United States that serve over 10 million students. These schools are important because they provide affordable education and job training close to where people live. Community colleges also help the US economy by contributing nearly 900 billion dollars each year.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes the importance of Community College Month in April two thousand twenty five. It celebrates the more than one thousand community colleges across the United States that help people access higher education and job training. These colleges support and improve the economic success of our country.

sections.title: Facts About Community Colleges
sections.text: The first public community college opened in nineteen oh one in Illinois. It was called Joliet Junior College and started with just six students. The school was created to help people get a college education without having to leave their hometown. The name community college became popular in nineteen forty seven when a government commission used it to describe these local schools. Community leaders across the country built many community colleges over several decades to give people in their areas better chances for education and jobs. Today there are one thousand twenty six community colleges in America. This includes nine hundred twenty one public colleges, thirty six colleges run by Native American tribes, and sixty nine private colleges. These schools serve ten million two hundred thousand students taking classes for credit and students taking classes that do not count toward a degree. Public community colleges are affordable. Students who live in the same area as the college pay an average of three thousand nine hundred ninety dollars per year in tuition and fees. The average student age is twenty seven years old. Many students work jobs, have low incomes, are parents, are military veterans, or are the first person in their family to attend college. Community colleges are the main schools that train people for jobs in the United States. They teach students skills for many different careers including computer chip making, construction, nursing, and other healthcare jobs. During the two thousand twenty two to two thousand twenty three school year, community colleges taught forty three point one percent of all undergraduate students in America. Most community college students live close to their school. The typical distance from a student's home to their community college is only ten miles. Eighty two percent of public high schools offer dual enrollment programs. These programs let high school students take college classes, usually at their local community college. This helps students save time and money when they go to college and lets them explore possible careers. People who graduated from community colleges earned eight hundred ninety eight billion five hundred million dollars in extra income for the national economy in fiscal year two thousand twenty. This amount equals four point one percent of the total gross domestic product of the United States. Every dollar the government spends on community colleges brings back six dollars and eighty cents in tax money. Celebrating Community College Month in April two thousand twenty five gives Americans a chance to recognize the value of schools that have long supported the success, competitiveness, and community strength of the United States and will be important to America's economic future.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.text: Therefore, the House of Representatives recognizes the importance of Community College Month as a chance to celebrate the over one thousand community colleges throughout the United States. These colleges serve local and regional needs and make the economy of the United States stronger.
